title: Reconcile requested Python/Django + plain JS stack change with supported ICP stack

requirements.text: Treat the user request to change the frontend to plain HTML/CSS/JavaScript and the backend to Python/Django as a non-actionable request within this project, and add a clear, user-facing notice in the UI (footer) stating that the deployed application runs on the Internet Computer with a Motoko backend and a React-based frontend.

quotes: their is sum big change  frontand : html,css,java script backend:python,django

acceptanceCriteria: The footer displays an English sentence indicating the app uses a Motoko backend on the Internet Computer and a React frontend.
No references to Python or Django are added anywhere in the UI.
The app builds and renders without runtime errors.

constraints: Do not attempt to migrate the backend to Python/Django; only Motoko is supported for backend code in this project.
Do not replace the React/TypeScript frontend with a non-React HTML/CSS/JS app; the frontend stack is fixed to React + TypeScript + Tailwind + React Query.
Keep all backend logic (if any is added later) in the single Motoko actor at backend/main.mo.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui or any frontend immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Implementing or deploying a Django project.
Adding Python code or Django REST endpoints.
Rebuilding the frontend as a non-React static site.
